name: Document Drafting Workflow
description: Structure, draft, and refine a professional document such as a policy, proposal, report, or specification

inputs.name: document_type
inputs.description: Type of document: policy, proposal, specification, report, memo, or brief

inputs.name: subject
inputs.description: The topic or purpose the document addresses

inputs.name: audience
inputs.description: Intended readers: executives, technical team, regulators, customers, or general staff

inputs.name: key_points
inputs.description: Main points, requirements, or arguments the document must cover

inputs.name: reference_docs
inputs.description: Existing documents, templates, or standards to base the draft on

inputs.name: length
inputs.description: Approximate target length: brief (1 page), standard (2-5 pages), or detailed (5+ pages)

outputs.name: document_draft
outputs.description: Full draft of the requested document with appropriate structure and formatting

outputs.name: revision_notes
outputs.description: Notes on assumptions made and areas that may need subject-matter-expert review

steps: Confirm the document type, subject, audience, and key points with the user

steps.if: reference documents or templates were provided
then: Read the reference materials and extract relevant structure, tone, and content requirements

steps: Propose a document outline (sections and subsections) and confirm it with the user before drafting

then: Draft each section in sequence, maintaining consistent tone and formatting
Add an executive summary or abstract at the top if the document is longer than two pages
Include a revision notes section flagging assumptions and areas needing SME input
else: Revise the outline based on user feedback and re-confirm before drafting

steps: Present the complete draft to the user for review

then: Apply the requested revisions section by section
Re-present the updated draft and ask if further changes are needed
